operating_state: Consolidated sales reached $24.8 billion for the first six months of 2021, with Q2 sales increasing 29% driven by higher end-user demand across all regions and segments. Order backlog increased $1.5 billion to $18.4 billion by quarter-end, supported by favorable dealer inventory impacts as dealers decreased inventories less than the prior year period.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin expanded to 14.5% for the six-month period from 10.6% prior year, with Q2 margin reaching 13.9% from 7.8% driven by higher sales volume and improved operational efficiency. Manufacturing costs increased due to higher period manufacturing and material costs, partially offset by favorable cost absorption, while operating expenses rose significantly driven by reinstatement of short-term incentive compensation expense after 2020 suspension.
material_events: Caterpillar acquired the Oil & Gas division of Weir Group PLC for $361 million in February 2021 and reinstated short-term incentive compensation plans after COVID-19 suspension. The Board declared an 8% dividend increase to $1.11 per share in June and resumed share repurchases. The company ended Q2 with $10.8 billion of enterprise cash and generated $4.0 billion in operating cash flow for the six-month period.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring supply chain disruptions and associated cost and labor pressures, with particular focus on semiconductors, transportation and raw materials. Contingency plans have been developed to minimize supply chain challenges that may impact the ability to meet increasing customer demand, while COVID-19 variants represent an ongoing area of monitoring.
forward_outlook: Management expects approximately $250 million of restructuring costs in 2021 with prior restructuring actions providing an incremental benefit of about $200 million to operating costs. Short-term incentive compensation expense is expected to be approximately $1.5 billion for full-year 2021, while the company maintains its strategy to return substantially all free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases while preserving its mid-A credit rating.
uncertainty_profile: Supply chain conditions remain fluid with ongoing monitoring of semiconductor availability, transportation constraints and raw material costs, while COVID-19 variants and their potential operational impacts represent ongoing areas of assessment. The company maintains $10.5 billion in global credit facilities with covenant compliance well above required thresholds, and Financial Products maintains adequate covenant ratios with interest coverage and leverage ratios comfortably within required limits.
